goal: Port structural.god_module to v2: aggregate over Structure.callables() grouped by file scope (top-level only); no view-method or Language vocabulary expansion needed

summary.title: Port structural.god_module + fix linter/rules import cycle
summary.what: run_god_module_v2 iterates Structure.callables() and Structure.scopes() directly, counting top-level definitions per file by checking each record's parent against the set of FILE/MODULE/PACKAGE-kind scope qualnames. Class-nested methods are excluded (their parent is a CLASS-kind scope) which keeps god-module distinct from god-class. No view-method, no Language vocabulary expansion. Parity verified against legacy god_module_kernel across 6 fixtures including the Java god-class case (1 class wrapping 25 methods → god-module count = 1). Zero mismatches. 10 new tests; 515 tests pass total. Also fixed a pre-existing latent import cycle between slop.linter.__init__ and the three rules-package __init__ files via lazy module-level __getattr__ — registries materialise on first access.
summary.why: Two motivations bundled because the god_module test surfaced the cycle: (1) the god_module port is the 4th rule to go view-native, proves a different pattern from cyclomatic/cognitive (aggregate over callables/scopes directly, no per-callable AST walk, no view-method needed); (2) the cycle was masked by full-suite import order but blew up when running any rule's test file in isolation, including the pre-existing test_god_module.py. Better to fix it now than leave a foot-gun that hides until someone adds an isolated test.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.god_module to v2: aggregate over Structure.callables() grouped by file scope (top-level only); no view-method or Language vocabulary expansion needed

decisions.chose: Pivot from npath to god_module after surfacing npath's substantial Language API expansion.
decisions.rationale: npath needs ~9 new Language classmethods (if_node, else_clause, elif_clause, loop_nodes, switch_node, case_node, try_node, catch_node, block_types) because its algorithm is per-language-shape, not per-language-data like cyclomatic/cognitive. That's a substantial API surface (99 method additions across 11 grammars) deserving its own scoped conversation. god_module is a smaller, cleaner port that proves a different pattern.

decisions.chose: No view-method for god_module — iterate Structure.callables() and Structure.scopes() directly in the rule.
decisions.rationale: god_module is an aggregate over records; no per-callable AST walk is needed. Adding Structure.god_module_count(path) would be useless ceremony — the rule's logic IS aggregation, not metric computation. Proves the 'view-native rule without a view-method' pattern.

decisions.chose: Filter top-level via parent-in-{module-scope-qualnames}, not by string comparison against file stems.
decisions.rationale: ScopeKind.FILE/MODULE/PACKAGE captures the language-specific reality (Python: FILE scope; Go: PACKAGE; Rust: MODULE). Iterating scopes once for the module-qualnames set is O(scopes), then O(1) membership for each callable/scope. Correctly handles Java where ALL methods are nested in a class.

decisions.chose: Lazy __getattr__ on slop.linter.__init__ for the aggregated registries.
decisions.rationale: Pre-existing cycle: each rules package imports legacy_v2_shim from slop.linter._shim (which forces slop.linter.__init__), and slop.linter.__init__ was eagerly importing STRUCTURAL_RULES etc. from those rules packages. Lazy __getattr__ defers the rules import until first attribute access, by which point the packages have finished their own __init__. Smaller fix than moving legacy_v2_shim to slop._shim.

decisions.chose: Keep run_god_module (legacy entry function) in the file, alongside run_god_module_v2.
decisions.rationale: Some tests still call it via the legacy import path. Removing now would force a tests-test_rules update that doesn't belong in this intent. Will retire alongside other legacy entries when the kernel deletion sweep starts.

decisions.chose: Exclude CallableKind.LAMBDA from the top-level count.
decisions.rationale: Lambdas are inline values, not definitions. A file with 25 lambda-assignments is not a god-module candidate; the count should be 0. Legacy god_module_kernel had this distinction implicitly via its function-vs-lambda discrimination at the kernel level.

review_notes: 515 tests pass (was 505). 10 new v2 god_module rule tests + the previously-broken-in-isolation test_god_module.py now passes when run alone (was: ImportError on cycle).

Parity verified directly against legacy god_module_kernel on 6 fixtures: 25 top-level funcs, few funcs, 22 top-level classes, mixed funcs+classes, Java class with 25 methods, Go 23 funcs. Zero mismatches.
The Java god-class case is the discriminating test: legacy and v2 BOTH report count=1 (the wrapping class), not count=25 (the nested methods). god-class is the WMC concern, not god-module's.
Port status: 4 view-native (cyclomatic, cognitive, god_module, imposters), 27 shim-wrapped.
npath remains shim-wrapped pending design conversation about expanding Language with the 9 NPath-specific classmethods.

architectural_claims: A v2 rule may iterate structure.callables() and structure.scopes() directly without a per-rule view method when its work is aggregation over records, not per-callable metric computation.
Top-level discrimination in v2 uses ScopeKind (FILE/MODULE/PACKAGE for module-level, CLASS/INTERFACE/STRUCT/TRAIT/IMPL for class-level), not string parsing of qualnames.

slop.linter.__init__ exports its aggregated registries via module-level __getattr__; the registries materialise on first access. This pattern keeps the cycle between slop.linter and the rules packages dissolved.
behavioral_changes: structural.god_module rule runs view-native; identical counts to legacy across the test corpus.
test_god_module.py (legacy test) now runs in isolation without ImportError (previously masked by full-suite import order).
